{{Quote|This is a day of celebration. Together with our friend the Emperor, Sullust will march into the prosperous new galaxy the Imperials envision. Join with us, and enter a better tomorrow.|Chairman Siin Suub's SoroSuub Corporation Proclamation 137d|<ref name="The Star Wars Sourcebook">''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''</ref>}}
'''Siin Suub''', sometimes styled as '''Sin Suub''', was a [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a board member of the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]]. During his [[Time/Legends|time]] as [[Chairman/Legends|Chairman]] of SoroSuub, the company overthrew and replaced the government of [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] and pledged allegiance to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Chairman Suub was also known to use industrial [[Spy/Legends|spies]].

==Biography==
Siin Suub, sometimes spelled as "Sin Suub," was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]]<ref name="s" /> who had a common Sullustan surname that meant "wainwright."<ref name="GCG">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Suub was a bureaucrat working as the [[Chairman/Legends|Chairman]] of the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] as of [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] (or Sullustan [[Standard year/Legends|year]] 8,494). As such, he had an almost complete, though unofficial, control over his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], because his company employed half the native population.<ref name="s" /> Through his career, Suub employed several industrial [[Spy/Legends|spies]], [[Vacander]] being among his favorites.<ref name="a">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>

On the sixty-second [[Standard day/Legends|day]] of the same year, Chairman Suub approved [[Corporate Proclamation 137d]], declaring that SoroSuub was aligned with and supporting the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and denouncing the Empire's enemy, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. The same Proclamation also declared that the SoroSuub Corporation was taking over Sullust, and thus what was previously said about SoroSuub was also applied to Sullust.<ref name="s" />

Nevertheless, Sullustans did not become strong supporters of the Empire and, even as Suub was making his statement, many members of that species fought for the Rebel Alliance, earning important distinction in that organization.<ref name="s" />

==Behind the scenes==
"Siin Suub" was first mentioned in ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''. That book's [[The Star Wars Sourcebook (Second Edition)|second edition]] and ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' mention him as "Sin Suub." However, [[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']] uses the original spelling of "Siin." As ''Millennium Falcon'', published in [[2008]], is the most recent [[canon]]ical source, this article uses the most current spelling of his name.
